task_name: Add JSDoc comments to packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts
description: Add JSDoc documentation comments to the main entry file packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts. Add a file-level JSDoc comment describing the module purpose, and add brief JSDoc comments to any exported functions, classes, or constants. Keep it concise.

task_name: Clear the SQLite database
description: Delete the SQLite database file at the project root: talent-front-door.db. The DB path is configured in packages/mcp-server/src/config.ts (DB_PATH env var or defaults to 'talent-front-door.db'). Simply remove the file so it gets recreated fresh on next server start. Do NOT modify any source code — just delete the .db file. Commit the deletion.

task_name: Replace generic emojis with Gusto-aligned icons on IC and PE dashboards
description: Replace the generic Unicode emojis used on the IC and PE task dashboards with icons that are more inline with the Gusto brand.

task_name: Remove all emojis and icons from task cards
description: Remove all emoji and icon rendering from the task cards on the IC and PE dashboards.

task_name: Fix inconsistent StatusTag formatting on dashboard task cards
description: Fix a layout bug in the IC/PE task dashboard where the StatusTag wraps to two lines.

task_name: Remove all emojis from QPR dashboard task cards
description: Remove all emoji icons from the QPR task dashboard cards. The emojis (trophy, writing hand, speech bubble, star, people) appear on task card headers. They exist in THREE files: FILE 1: packages/mcp-apps/src/shared/components.tsx - DELETE the entire TASK_ICONS constant (~line 118-124, a Record mapping task names to Unicode emoji escape sequences like \u270D\uFE0F) - DELETE the line: const icon = TASK_ICONS[task.task] || "" - DELETE the JSX element: {icon} (in the TaskCard title area) - Also check around lines ~1486-1534 for any icon prop usage and remove emoji spans there too FILE 2: packages/mcp-apps/src/review-preview/mcp-app.tsx - DELETE the entire TASK_ICONS constant (~lines 35-39) - DELETE the line: const icon = TASK_ICONS[data.task_type] || "" - DELETE the JSX element: {icon} FILE 3: packages/mcp-server/src/artifacts/task-dashboard.tsx - DELETE the TASK_ICONS constant (~lines 108-112, same emoji mappings as the other files) - DELETE any line that reads from TASK_ICONS (e.g. const icon = TASK_ICONS[...]) - DELETE any {icon} in the JSX that renders these emojis IMPORTANT: Do NOT remove other Unicode characters like checkmarks (\u2714), arrows (\u2192 \u203A), dots (\u00B7), or dashes (\u2014). Those are functional UI elements. ONLY remove the colorful emoji icons (the ones in TASK_ICONS maps). After changes, card headers should show just the task name text with no icon. Commit to a branch and open a PR.

task_name: Add JSDoc to draft-store.ts data layer
description:
Add JSDoc documentation to packages/mcp-server/src/data/draft-store.ts. This is the core data persistence layer for QPR drafts.

Add a file-level JSDoc comment explaining that this module manages SQLite-backed draft storage for self-reflections, upward feedback, and PE evaluations.

Add JSDoc comments to EACH of these exported functions:
- validateTransition(from, to) — explain it enforces the draft status state machine
- assertEditable(draft) — explain it throws if draft is in a non-editable state
- safeJsonParse(str) — explain it returns parsed JSON or null on failure
- rowToDraft(row) — explain it converts a SQLite row to a Draft object
- saveDraft(...) — explain params: employeeId, cycleId, taskType, sections, reportId
- loadDraft(...) — explain it returns a Draft or undefined
- getDraftsForUser(employeeId, cycleId) — explain it returns all drafts for a user in a cycle
- isSubmittedToWorkday(draft) — explain what 'submitted' means in the state machine
- deleteDraft(...) — explain this is admin-only for testing

Keep JSDoc concise — 1-2 sentences per function plus @param and @returns tags. Do NOT modify any logic.

task_name: Extract Glean config constants from collect-work-wins.ts
description:
Extract hardcoded Glean API configuration values from packages/mcp-server/src/tools/router/collect-work-wins.ts into the existing config module at packages/mcp-server/src/config.ts.

Currently hardcoded values in collect-work-wins.ts:
1. Glean auth path (~line 14-18): ~/.mcp-auth/mcp-remote-0.1.37/ — extract as GLEAN_AUTH_PATH with env var override
2. Page size (~line 59): pageSize: 20 — extract as GLEAN_PAGE_SIZE constant
3. Request timeout (~line 76): 30_000 — extract as GLEAN_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS constant
4. Glean search URL (GLEAN_SEARCH_URL variable) — make configurable via env var

In config.ts, add a new 'glean' section to the existing config pattern:
```
export const GLEAN_AUTH_PATH = process.env.GLEAN_AUTH_PATH || path.join(os.homedir(), '.mcp-auth/mcp-remote-0.1.37/');
export const GLEAN_PAGE_SIZE = 20;
export const GLEAN_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000;
export const GLEAN_SEARCH_URL = process.env.GLEAN_SEARCH_URL || 'https://gusto-be.glean.com/api/v1/search';
```

Then update collect-work-wins.ts to import these constants from config.ts instead of using inline values. Do NOT change any logic — just extract the magic values.

task_name: Add JSDoc to utility modules (word-count, sanitize, resolve-identity)
description:
Add JSDoc documentation to three utility files in packages/mcp-server/src/utils/:

1. word-count.ts — Add JSDoc to:
   - countWords(text) — counts words in a string
   - validateWordCount(text, max) — checks if text exceeds word limit
   - optimizeWordCount(text, section, maxWords) — trims text to fit limit
   Add @param and @returns tags. Note the 300-word QPR section limit in the module doc.

2. sanitize.ts — Add JSDoc to:
   - sanitizeForWorkday(text) — strips/escapes text for Workday API submission
   - parseStopStartContinue(text) — parses qualitative feedback into stop/start/continue sections
   Include @example tags showing input/output for parseStopStartContinue.

3. resolve-identity.ts — Add JSDoc to:
   - resolveIdentity(email, callerEmail, profile) — explain the identity resolution chain: override email > caller email > profile email > GMS lookup
   Document the security audit logging behavior (console.warn when email override is used).

Keep each JSDoc concise (1-3 sentences + tags). Do NOT modify any logic.

task_name: Create .env.example documenting all environment variables
description:
Create a .env.example file at the project root that documents all environment variables used across the codebase. Search for process.env references in all .ts files under packages/mcp-server/src/.

Known env vars to document (grep for more):
- DB_PATH — SQLite database file path (default: 'talent-front-door.db') — from config.ts
- NODE_ENV — runtime environment (development/production/test) — from config.ts
- VITEST — set automatically during tests — from config.ts
- USE_WORKDAY_MCP — enable real Workday MCP integration — from config.ts
- BYPASS_EMAILS — comma-separated list of emails that bypass restrictions — from config.ts
- GLEAN_SEARCH_URL — Glean API endpoint — from collect-work-wins.ts
- MCP_REMOTE_AUTH_DIR or similar — Glean auth token path — from collect-work-wins.ts

Format each variable with a comment explaining its purpose, default value, and whether it's required or optional:
```
# SQLite database path (optional, default: talent-front-door.db)
# DB_PATH=talent-front-door.db

# Runtime environment (optional, default: development)
# NODE_ENV=development
```

Also add .env.example to .gitignore if .env is not already there. Do NOT create or modify an actual .env file.

task_name: Export Glean API types from collect-work-wins.ts
description:
Export the Glean API interfaces defined in packages/mcp-server/src/tools/router/collect-work-wins.ts so they can be reused by other modules.

Currently these interfaces are defined but NOT exported (~lines 23-38):
- GleanDocument — represents a Glean search result document
- GleanResult — represents a single search result with document and snippets
- GleanSearchResult — represents the full Glean API response

Changes:
1. Add 'export' keyword to each interface definition
2. Move these interfaces to a new file: packages/mcp-server/src/data/types-glean.ts
3. Update collect-work-wins.ts to import them from the new file
4. Add a brief JSDoc comment to each interface explaining what it represents

Alternatively, if moving to a new file feels too heavy, just add 'export' to the existing definitions in collect-work-wins.ts and add JSDoc. Either approach is fine.

Do NOT change any logic — just make the types available for import.

task_name: Replace any types in test files with proper typing
description:
Replace TypeScript `any` type assertions in test files with proper types.

Search for `: any` across all files in packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/. Common patterns to fix:

1. `(t: any) => t.task` — used in array .find() and .filter() calls for task results
   Fix: Import DashboardTask from '../../data/types' and use it, e.g. `(t: DashboardTask) => t.task`

2. `(r: any) => r.name` — used for direct report results
   Fix: Create or import a proper type for the report response shape

3. `result as any` — used to access nested response properties
   Fix: Create interface for the tool response shape

Approach:
- First, check packages/mcp-server/src/data/types.ts to see what types are already exported
- For any missing types, add them to types.ts
- Update test files to import and use proper types instead of `any`
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify no type errors are introduced

Focus on the most common patterns. If there are more than ~15 `any` occurrences, fix at least the 10 most impactful ones.

task_name: Add JSDoc to all tool router files (self-reflection group)
description:

2. draft-contributions.ts — Document: purpose (drafts Contributions & Impact section), the STAR/SBI framework requirement, the 300-word limit, how user types (metrics/axes/general) affect output structure
3. draft-looking-ahead.ts — Document: purpose (drafts Looking Ahead section), the 2-3 focus areas structure, AI amplification strategy requirement
4. set-ai-fluency.ts — Document: purpose (records AI fluency stage), the 5 stages (observer through amplifier), the coaching prompt return
5. save-self-reflection-draft.ts — Document: purpose (incremental draft save), the merge behavior with existing sections
6. submit-self-reflection.ts — Document: purpose (validates and moves to pending_submission), prerequisites, next step (confirm_submission)
7. preview-self-reflection.ts — Document: purpose (formatted preview without submitting)

For each file, add:
- File-level JSDoc explaining the tool's role in the self-reflection workflow
- JSDoc on the main exported function with @param and @returns
- Keep it concise: 1-3 sentences per function

Do NOT modify any logic.

For each file, add file-level JSDoc and JSDoc on the main exported function with @param and @returns. Keep concise. Do NOT modify any logic.

task_name: Add ARCHITECTURE.md documenting the project structure
description:
Create an ARCHITECTURE.md file at the project root that documents the high-level structure of the talent-front-door-mcp codebase.

Read through the directory structure and key files to understand the architecture, then write a concise doc covering:

1. Overview: What this project is (QPR Quarterly Performance Review MCP server + client apps for Gusto)
2. Package structure: Explain the monorepo layout: packages/mcp-server/ (the MCP server with tools, data layer, utilities) and packages/mcp-apps/ (React client apps rendered as MCP artifacts)
3. MCP Server architecture: src/index.ts (tool registration and request routing), src/tools/router/ (individual tool implementations, one file per tool), src/data/ (data access layer with draft-store.ts for SQLite, workday-client.ts, types.ts, GMS mock data), src/utils/ (shared utilities for word-count, sanitize, logger, identity resolution, user-type routing), src/artifacts/ (server-side fallback HTML artifacts)
4. MCP Apps architecture: src/shared/ (shared components like TaskCard, StatusTag, ProgressBar, plus tokens and constants), src/dashboard/ (IC/PE task dashboard), src/self-reflection/ (self-reflection workflow app), src/upward-feedback/ (upward feedback workflow app), src/pe-evaluation/ (PE evaluation workflow app), src/review-preview/ (review preview cards)
5. Key workflows: Brief description of the 3 QPR workflows (self-reflection, upward feedback, PE evaluation) and their tool chains
6. Data flow: Draft lifecycle: draft -> editing -> pending_submission -> submitted_to_workday
7. Configuration: Point to config.ts and .env.example for environment variables

Keep it concise — aim for 150-200 lines. Use markdown headers and brief descriptions, not exhaustive details.

task_name: Add unit tests for word-count utility
description:
Create unit tests for packages/mcp-server/src/utils/word-count.ts.

Create the test file at: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/unit/word-count.test.ts

Test the following functions:

1. countWords(text): empty string returns 0, single word returns 1, multiple words with single spaces, multiple spaces between words (should not double-count), leading/trailing whitespace, newlines and tabs as separators, text with punctuation (hello world = 2 words)

2. validateWordCount(text, max): text under limit returns valid, text at exact limit returns valid, text over limit returns invalid with count, empty text returns valid

3. optimizeWordCount(text, section, maxWords): text under limit returns unchanged, text over limit gets trimmed, verify trimmed text is under the limit, section name appears in any messaging/metadata

Use the existing test framework (vitest). Check packages/mcp-server/vitest.config.ts and existing test files for the test setup pattern.

Run the tests with: npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/word-count.test.ts

task_name: Add unit tests for sanitize utility
description:
Create unit tests for packages/mcp-server/src/utils/sanitize.ts.

Create the test file at: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/unit/sanitize.test.ts

Test the following functions:

1. sanitizeForWorkday(text): plain text passes through unchanged, HTML tags are stripped or escaped, script tags are removed, empty string returns empty string, special characters (quotes, ampersands, angle brackets)

2. parseStopStartContinue(text): text with clear Stop/Start/Continue sections, text with different casing (STOP, stop, Stop), text with only some sections present (e.g., missing Stop), text with no recognizable sections, empty string, text with extra whitespace around section headers

Read the actual implementation first to understand what each function does, then write tests that match the actual behavior.

Use vitest. Run with: npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/sanitize.test.ts

task_name: Add unit tests for draft-store state machine transitions
description:
Create unit tests for the draft state machine in packages/mcp-server/src/data/draft-store.ts.

Create the test file at: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/unit/draft-store.test.ts

Focus on testing these functions:

1. validateTransition(from, to): all valid transitions succeed (read the implementation to find the valid transition map), invalid transitions throw or return false, transition from undefined/null (new draft) to initial state, transition from editing to pending_submission, transition from pending_submission to submitted_to_workday, transition backwards (e.g., submitted_to_workday to editing) should fail

2. assertEditable(draft): draft in editing state does not throw, draft in pending_submission state throws, draft in submitted_to_workday state throws, null/undefined draft handling

3. isSubmittedToWorkday(draft): returns true for submitted drafts, returns false for editing/pending drafts, returns false for null/undefined

4. safeJsonParse(str): valid JSON returns parsed object, invalid JSON returns null, empty string returns null, null input returns null

Read the actual implementation first to understand the state machine transitions and error handling. Use vitest with the existing test setup.

Run with: npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/draft-store.test.ts

task_name: Add unit tests for formatters.ts
description:
Create unit tests for packages/mcp-server/src/utils/formatters.ts.

Create the test file at: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/unit/formatters.test.ts

This file contains 3 pure formatting functions that convert draft data into human-readable output:
1. formatSelfReflection(draft) - formats contributions, looking_ahead, ai_fluency sections into a preview string
2. formatUpwardFeedback(draft) - formats ratings (Likert 1-5) and qualitative (stop/start/continue) into a preview string
3. formatEvaluation(draft) - formats PE evaluation with rating, contributions, looking_ahead into a preview string

For each function, test:
- Complete draft with all sections filled -> properly formatted output
- Draft with missing optional sections -> graceful handling (no crashes)
- Empty sections object -> returns something reasonable
- Ratings as numbers vs strings (formatUpwardFeedback handles both)

Read the actual implementation first to understand the exact output format, then write tests matching it.
Use vitest. Run with: cd packages/mcp-server && npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/formatters.test.ts

task_name: Add unit tests for level-guidance.ts
description:
Create unit tests for packages/mcp-server/src/utils/level-guidance.ts.

Create the test file at: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/unit/level-guidance.test.ts

This file provides job-level-specific calibration guidance for QPR reviews. Test:
1. getLevelGuidance(level) with senior levels (L6+) - should return senior-tier guidance
2. getLevelGuidance(level) with mid levels (L4-L5) - should return mid-tier guidance
3. getLevelGuidance(level) with junior levels (L1-L3) - should return junior-tier guidance
4. getLevelGuidance with unknown/invalid level string - should return a default or fallback
5. getLevelGuidance with empty string or undefined

Read the implementation first to understand the tier boundaries and output shape.
Use vitest. Run with: cd packages/mcp-server && npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/level-guidance.test.ts

task_name: Add unit tests for resolve-identity.ts
description:
Create unit tests for packages/mcp-server/src/utils/resolve-identity.ts.

Create the test file at: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/unit/resolve-identity.test.ts

This is a security-critical module that resolves caller identity through a chain: override email > caller email > profile email > GMS lookup.

Test resolveIdentity(email, callerEmail, profile):
1. With override email provided and in BYPASS_EMAILS list -> uses override email
2. With override email NOT in BYPASS_EMAILS list -> ignores override, falls through
3. With callerEmail provided -> resolves to that employee
4. With profile.email provided -> resolves to that employee
5. With no identity sources -> returns error object
6. With email that doesn't match any GMS employee -> returns error

You will need to mock the config module for BYPASS_EMAILS. Check how existing tests mock config (look at packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/ for patterns).

Use vitest. Run with: cd packages/mcp-server && npx vitest run src/__tests__/unit/resolve-identity.test.ts

task_name: Create .nvmrc and .editorconfig files

2. .editorconfig - Create with standard settings:

```
[*]
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2
end_of_line = lf
charset = utf-8
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
insert_final_newline = true

[*.md]

```

These are new files only. Do NOT modify any existing files.

task_name: Enhance README.md with Prerequisites and Project Structure sections
description:
Enhance the existing README.md at the project root by adding two new sections. Insert them AFTER the existing Quick Start section.

1. Prerequisites section:
- Node.js >= 20.0.0 (see .nvmrc)
- npm (comes with Node.js)
- SQLite3 (used for draft storage, auto-created on first run)

2. Project Structure section:
Brief description of the monorepo layout:
- packages/mcp-server/ - The MCP server: tool implementations, data layer, utilities
- packages/mcp-apps/ - React client apps rendered as MCP artifacts (dashboard, self-reflection, upward feedback, PE evaluation, review preview)
- packages/shared/ - Shared constants (Gusto values)
- knowledge/ - QPR framework templates and calibration guidance
- docs/plans/ - Implementation planning documents

3. Running Tests section:
```
npm test                    # Run all tests
npm run typecheck           # Type-check without building
cd packages/mcp-server && npx vitest run  # Run server tests directly
cd packages/mcp-server && npx vitest       # Watch mode
```

Keep it concise. Do NOT remove or rewrite existing sections — only ADD new ones.

task_name: Replace non-null assertions with optional chaining in task-dashboard.tsx
description:
In packages/mcp-server/src/artifacts/task-dashboard.tsx, replace unsafe non-null assertions with optional chaining.

Find these patterns (around lines 118-119, 146):
- task.details!.filter(...) -> task.details?.filter(...) ?? []
- task.details!.length -> task.details?.length ?? 0
- task.details!.map(...) -> (task.details ?? []).map(...)

The code already has a hasReports guard, but the non-null assertions are unnecessary and optional chaining is cleaner.

This is a type-only change — the rendered output should be identical.
Run npx tsc --noEmit to verify no type errors.

task_name: Type-safe UPWARD_FEEDBACK_LABELS using UPWARD_FEEDBACK_KEYS
Create GitHub Actions workflow for tests
description:
Create a GitHub Actions CI workflow at .github/workflows/test.yml that runs tests on PRs.

The workflow should:
1. Trigger on: push to main, pull_request to main
2. Use Node.js 20
3. Run: npm ci
4. Run: npm run typecheck (if available, otherwise tsc --build --force)
5. Run: npm test

Keep it simple — single job, ubuntu-latest runner. Example:
```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run typecheck
      - run: npm test
```

Create the .github/workflows/ directory if it doesn't exist.
This is a new file only — does not modify any existing code.

task_name: Fix StepIndicator to prevent navigating to future incomplete steps
description:
In the MCP apps, the StepIndicator component allows users to click on future (incomplete) steps to navigate to them. While the submit button validates completeness, this is a UX concern — users shouldn't be able to jump ahead to steps they haven't reached yet.

Search for StepIndicator across all files in packages/mcp-apps/src/. Find the component definition and its click handler.

The fix should:
1. Keep completed steps clickable (users can go back to review)
2. Keep the current step clickable
3. Disable clicking on future steps that haven't been completed yet
4. Visually indicate disabled steps (e.g., reduced opacity, cursor: default instead of pointer)

Look at how the component tracks current step and completed steps, then add a condition to the onClick handler that prevents navigation to steps beyond the furthest completed step + 1.

This is a UX improvement — the app still functions correctly without it (submit validates), but it prevents user confusion.

Run npx tsc --noEmit to verify no type errors.

task_name: Add explicit null to useRef type annotations in MCP apps
description:
In the MCP apps React components, some useRef calls don't explicitly include null in their type annotation. While this works fine with React 19 and compiles clean, it's better practice to be explicit.

Search for useRef across all files in packages/mcp-apps/src/.

For each useRef call, ensure the type includes null:
- Before: const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
- After: const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement | null>(null)

Or if using useRef without an initial value:
- Before: const ref = useRef<SomeType>()
- After: const ref = useRef<SomeType | null>(null)

This is a type-only change — no runtime behavior change. It makes the null possibility explicit in the type system, which helps catch potential null access bugs.

Run npx tsc --noEmit to verify no type errors after changes.

task_name: Add JSDoc to formatters.ts utility
description:
Add JSDoc documentation to packages/mcp-server/src/utils/formatters.ts.

This file contains pure formatting functions that convert draft data into human-readable preview strings for the review/submit flow.

Add:
1. File-level JSDoc explaining: This module provides formatting utilities that convert raw draft sections into human-readable preview strings for the QPR review workflow. Used by submit and preview tools.

2. JSDoc for formatSelfReflection(draft):
   - Purpose: Formats a self-reflection draft into a preview string
   - @param draft - The ReviewDraft containing contributions, looking_ahead, ai_fluency sections
   - @returns Formatted string with section headers and content

3. JSDoc for formatUpwardFeedback(draft):
   - Purpose: Formats upward feedback with Likert ratings and Stop/Start/Continue qualitative sections
   - @param draft - The ReviewDraft containing ratings (as JSON string) and qualitative sections
   - @returns Formatted string with rating scores and qualitative feedback

4. JSDoc for formatEvaluation(draft):
   - Purpose: Formats a PE evaluation with rating, contributions, and looking_ahead
   - @param draft - The ReviewDraft containing rating, contributions, looking_ahead sections
   - @returns Formatted string with rating badge and section content

Keep concise. Do NOT modify any logic.

task_name: Add JSDoc to level-guidance.ts and constants.ts
description:
Add JSDoc documentation to two files:

1. packages/mcp-server/src/utils/level-guidance.ts:
   - File-level JSDoc: Provides job-level-specific calibration guidance for QPR reviews. Maps job levels to tiers (senior/mid/junior) and returns appropriate expectations and examples.
   - Document getLevelGuidance(level): explains the 3 tiers, what determines tier placement, and what the returned guidance object contains
   - Add @param level - Job level string (e.g., 'L6', 'L4', 'L2')
   - Add @returns Object with tier-specific calibration guidance

2. packages/mcp-server/src/constants.ts:
   - File-level JSDoc: Central constants module for the QPR MCP server. Contains rating scales, AI fluency stages, draft statuses, upward feedback dimensions, and cycle configuration.
   - Add brief JSDoc to each exported constant explaining its purpose:
     - RATINGS: The three performance rating levels plus default
     - AI_FLUENCY_STAGES: The five AI fluency maturity stages
     - DRAFT_STATUSES: The draft lifecycle states
     - UPWARD_FEEDBACK_KEYS: The five Likert rating dimensions
     - UPWARD_FEEDBACK_LABELS: Human-readable labels for the rating dimensions
     - CYCLE_CONFIG: Current QPR cycle dates and deadlines

Keep concise. Do NOT modify any logic or values.

task_name: Create packages/mcp-apps/README.md documenting the app build process
description:
Create a README.md file at packages/mcp-apps/README.md documenting the MCP apps package.

The README should cover:

1. Overview: This package contains React client applications rendered as MCP artifacts in the QPR workflow. Each app is built as a self-contained HTML file using Vite.

2. Apps list:
   - dashboard - IC and PE task dashboard showing QPR progress
   - self-reflection - Multi-step self-reflection workflow (Work Wins -> Contributions -> AI Fluency -> Looking Ahead -> Review)
   - upward-feedback - Upward feedback workflow (Ratings -> Qualitative -> Review)
   - pe-evaluation - PE evaluation workflow (Select Report -> Draft -> Rating -> Review)
   - review-preview - Preview cards for submitted reviews

3. Build process: Explain the INPUT environment variable pattern used in vite.config.ts. Each app has its own build command (build:dashboard, build:self-reflection, etc.) that sets INPUT to the app's entry HTML file. The build:all script runs all of them sequentially.

4. Shared code: Explain packages/mcp-apps/src/shared/ contains shared React components (TaskCard, StatusTag, ProgressBar, StepIndicator), design tokens (tokens.ts), and TypeScript types.

5. Development: Note that these apps are served as MCP artifacts — they run inside the Claude desktop client, not in a standalone browser.

Keep it concise (50-80 lines).
